suburb, and a Kerala State Electricity Board employee, who fell to his
death at a bank branch in Kannur” என்றும் குறிப்பிட்டுள்ளது.

இரண்டு மூன்று நாட்களாக ஏடுகளில் வெளிவந்துள்ள
செய்திகளைப் பார்த்தால்,
• Over one lakh lorries stranded for want of cash.

• Unbearable queues, unbankable ATMs - Life across the State
was either hours of waiting or frantic rushing from one cash

• Thecommon man was the most hit at banks drowned in chaos
with hundreds waiting in queues inside and outside, and in
front of ATMs for long hours on Friday.

• The demonetisation of Rs. 500 and Rs. 1000 notes has made
life miserable for lorry owners and drivers with over a lakh
lorries from Tamil Nadu remaining stranded all over the
• Business houses concede that they have not received their
consignments for the past three days.
• Caterers too are finding it difficult to pay vendors, daily wage

• Weekly wage workers in Tamil Nadu from sectors including
construction, plantations and MSME are hard hit. Many of
the workers went home emptyhanded on Saturday while
some got 50 per cent of their wages.

• Chennai, more than 30 per cent of employees in the
MSME sector were affected, according to sources tn the
industry.

• The demonetisation has had an adverse effect on the
operations of the Tamil film industry, which depends largely
on cash transactions for its daily operations.
